event: FPV camera supply from China (187/193 units, 97% market share) will face 8-12 week lead times as autonomous targeting platforms require ruggedized camera modules with AI-compatible outputs, creating procurement bottleneck for loitering munitions and interceptor programs.
drivers: Teledyne FLIR Prism SKR upgrade expanding to FPV drones and loitering munitions creates new camera specification requirements
97% China dependency (187/193 units) in FPV cameras creates single-source vulnerability
Autonomous target recognition requires camera modules with higher frame rates and AI-compatible data outputs

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Autonomous targeting software (Prism SKR) expanding into FPV platforms creates new camera spec requirements incompatible with existing China-sourced commercial cameras

event: Drone operations software procurement will increase 220-280% in H2 2026 as AirHub's €4.4M raise signals consolidation of mission planning, compliance, and C2 into integrated platforms for defense and critical infrastructure customers.
drivers: AirHub Series A funding targeting defense, public safety, and critical infrastructure markets
Thailand border security MoU demonstrates integration of secure comms, AI analytics, and drone operations platforms
Counter-UAS and incident response workflows requiring unified mission planning and live video streaming

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Drone operations centers converging with counter-UAS mission requirements, creating demand for integrated planning and response software

event: ESC median price will reach $58-68 (29-51% increase from $45) by Q4 2026 as US defense contractors pivot from China suppliers (119/167 units, 71%) to Ukraine and Germany alternatives for NDAA compliance, straining limited production capacity.
drivers: China dominates ESC supply at 71% (119/167 units) with Ukraine at only 8% (13 units)
Autonomous FPV platforms and loitering munitions requiring NDAA-compliant ESCs for DoD procurement
Ukraine and Germany suppliers (17 total units) have insufficient capacity to absorb 30-40% demand shift

event: Frame procurement will shift 18-25% from China suppliers (236/377 units, 63%) to USA sources (114 units, 30%) by H1 2027 as counter-UAS interceptor programs and autonomous loitering munition contracts mandate US-origin airframes, increasing median price from $32 to $46-54.
drivers: Teledyne FLIR Prism SKR supporting interceptors and loitering munitions requires airframe supply chain compliance
Current China dominance at 63% (236/377) creates diversion risk for dual-use autonomous platforms
USA supplier base at 30% (114 units) has capacity to absorb 15-20% additional volume from commercial-to-defense pivot

event: Video analytics AI accelerators (currently 14 units, median $84) will face 16-20 week lead times by Q3 2026 as Thailand border security deployment and counter-UAS programs require AI-powered video processing at edge nodes, straining US and Israel supplier capacity.
drivers: Thailand MoU includes SmartOkO AI-powered video analytics requiring edge AI accelerators for real-time processing
AirHub drone operations center with live video streaming creates demand for distributed AI inference
Current AI accelerator inventory at only 14 units with US (9) and Israel (2) suppliers facing defense priority allocations

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Video analytics AI accelerators becoming critical path component as ISR platforms integrate real-time autonomous target recognition

event: Motor supply from China (origin data unavailable in parts summary) will face 30-40% tariff premium by Q4 2026 as autonomous targeting platforms and counter-UAS interceptors require traceable motor provenance, forcing procurement shift to higher-cost US and European suppliers.
drivers: Autonomous FPV drones and interceptors under Prism SKR requiring full component traceability for export control compliance
Counter-UAS platforms demanding ruggedized motors with verified supply chains for defense certification
Thailand border security and critical infrastructure applications creating secondary demand for compliant motor sources
